title: Rich Spanish Cod Paella
description: A festive Spanish dish featuring cod, butter, and steam, this paella is a nut-free friendly option for any occasion, with a resinous and chewy texture.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in a large paella pan or skillet over medium-high heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook until it is translucent, about 5 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and cook for 1 minute, until fragrant.
Add the cod pieces and cook until they are browned on all sides, about 5 minutes.
Add the smoked paprika and saffron threads, and stir to combine.
Add the uncooked white rice and stir to combine with the cod and spices.
Add the chicken broth, salt, and black pepper, and bring to a boil.
Reduce the heat to low, cover the pan, and simmer for 20 minutes, until the rice is cooked and the liquid is absorbed.
Stir in the unsalted butter until it is melted and the paella is creamy.
Serve the paella hot, garnished with lemon wedges if desired.
Ensure poultry is cooked to an internal temperature of 74°C / 165°F (juices run clear, no pink remaining) before serving.
